title: Australia Proprietary Company Registration (ASIC Form 201, Corporations Act 2001)
description: Register a new proprietary company limited by shares (the standard small-business incorporation vehicle, commonly styled 'Pty Ltd') on the Australian companies register operated by the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), under the Corporations Act 2001. Since the Business Registration Service (register.business.gov.au) launched, most applicants now lodge online through that service (which can bundle an ABN application alongside registration); the underlying field set both channels collect is the one ASIC's own Form 201, 'Application for registration as an Australian company', has published since 2011 and still lists as its regulatory form for this process. This document models that field set, scoped to the proprietary-company-limited-by-shares case — by far the most common path for an individual or small group incorporating a business — and leaves the public-company-specific governance sections (constitution lodgement, minimum three-director/office-hours requirements, ultimate-holding-company disclosure for regulated public companies) out of scope; see VERIFICATION.md.

source.documentRef: ASIC Form 201 — 'Application for registration as an Australian company' (Corporations Act 2001, s117)

verification.notes: Authored from ASIC's own regulatory-resources page confirming Form 201 remains the current form for this process, plus business.gov.au's live guidance on the modern online lodgement channel and fee (the Business Registration Service, register.business.gov.au). The Form 201 PDF itself (dated 17 January 2011 on its own footer, the edition still linked from ASIC's page) was not directly downloadable from asic.gov.au in this sandbox (the direct link redirected to an unrelated resource) and was instead retrieved from a long-established third-party form-library mirror (calculus.net.au) — a static PDF with a genuine text layer (not scanned), so its printed field labels and instructional text were extracted directly via pdfjs-dist's getTextContent(), without needing OCR or image rendering. Cross-checked against ASIC's own page description ('11-page form ... collects company details, officeholder appointments, and registered office information'), which matches the retrieved PDF's page count and section structure. The live online lodgement screen flow (register.business.gov.au, requiring myGovID/RAM authentication) was not reached — see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: stateOfRegistration
fields.label: State or Territory of registration
fields.sourceRef: Section 1 — 'I apply for registration of the company ... and nominate the State or Territory in which the company will be taken to be registered.'

fields.name: hasProposedCompanyName
fields.label: Does the company have a proposed company name?
fields.description: If no, the company's name on registration will simply be its Australian Company Number (ACN).
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 — 'Does the company have a proposed company name? Yes / No. ... The company name on registration will be its Australian Company Number (ACN).'

fields.name: proposedCompanyName
fields.label: Proposed company name
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 — 'If yes, the proposed company name is'

fields.name: companyLegalElement
fields.label: Legal element in the company name
fields.description: This schema is scoped to a proprietary company limited by shares, so the modelled choice is between the two label forms it may use.
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 — 'Tick the legal elements that apply: Pty. Ltd. / Proprietary Limited'

fields.name: isNameIdenticalToRegisteredBusinessName
fields.label: Is the proposed name identical to a registered business name?
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Is the proposed name identical to a registered business name(s)? Yes / No'

fields.name: identicalBusinessNameNumber
fields.label: Business number of the identical registered business name
fields.description: Declares that the applicant owns, or is registering the company for the owner(s) of, the identical business name.
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'I declare that I own, or am registering the company for the owner(s) of the identical business name(s) ... Business number, State/Territory of registration'

fields.name: registeredOfficeStreet
fields.label: Registered office — street address
fields.description: A PO Box cannot be used as the registered office address.
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Registered office ... You cannot use a PO Box address. Office, unit, level / Street number and Street name'

fields.name: doesCompanyOccupyRegisteredOffice
fields.label: Does the company occupy the registered-office premises?
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Does the company occupy the premises? Yes / No'

fields.name: occupierName
fields.label: Name of the occupier of the premises
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'If no, name of occupier'

fields.name: occupierConsentGiven
fields.label: Has the occupier consented in writing to the registered-office use?
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Occupier's consent ... The occupier of the premises has consented in writing to the use of the specified address as the address of the registered office of the company and has not withdrawn that consent.'

fields.name: isPrincipalPlaceSameAsRegisteredOffice
fields.label: Is the principal place of business the same as the registered office?
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Principal place of business. If same as registered office, write "as above".'

fields.name: principalPlaceStreet
fields.label: Principal place of business — street address
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Office, unit, level / Street number and Street name' (principal place of business block)

fields.name: principalPlaceSuburb
fields.label: Principal place of business — suburb/city
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Suburb/City' (principal place of business block)

fields.name: principalPlaceState
fields.label: Principal place of business — State/Territory
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'State/Territory' (principal place of business block)

fields.name: principalPlacePostcode
fields.label: Principal place of business — postcode
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 continued — 'Postcode' (principal place of business block)

fields.name: principalPlaceCountry
fields.label: Principal place of business — country, if not Australia

fields.name: director1FamilyName
fields.label: Director — family name
fields.description: A proprietary company must have a minimum of one director, resident in Australia.
fields.sourceRef: Section 3 — 'Appoint officeholder ... Office held: Director / Secretary ... Family name'

fields.name: director1Country
fields.label: Director — residential address, country (if not Australia)

fields.name: hasSecondDirector
fields.label: Are you appointing a second director?

fields.name: hasSecretary
fields.label: Are you appointing a company secretary?
fields.description: Optional for a proprietary company, but if appointed the secretary must reside in Australia.
fields.sourceRef: Section 3 — 'Office held: Director / Secretary' with guidance 'The office of secretary is optional, but if appointed one must reside in Australia.'

fields.name: shareClassCode
fields.label: Share class code
fields.description: ORD = ordinary shares, the overwhelmingly common case for a new proprietary company; other standard codes exist for special share structures.
fields.sourceRef: Section 5 — 'Standard share codes: A, B, ORD (ordinary), PRF (preference), RED (redeemable), SPE (special), EMP (employee's), FOU (founder's), LG (life governor's), MAN (management), CUMP, NCP, REDP, NRP, CRP, NCRP, PARP'

fields.name: amountPaidPerShare
fields.label: Total amount paid on these shares
fields.sourceRef: Section 5 — 'Total amount paid on these shares'

fields.name: amountUnpaidPerShare
fields.label: Total amount unpaid on these shares
fields.sourceRef: Section 5 — 'Total amount unpaid on these shares'

fields.name: areSharesIssuedOtherThanForCash
fields.label: Are any of these shares issued other than for cash?
fields.sourceRef: Section 5 — 'Are any of these shares issued other than for cash? Yes / No'

fields.name: member1GivenNames
fields.label: Member — given names
fields.description: Either an individual member's given/family names, or a company member's name — mutually exclusive with member1CompanyName.

fields.name: member1CompanyName
fields.label: Member — company name (if the member is a company)

fields.name: member1AddressStreet
fields.label: Member — address, street
fields.sourceRef: Section 6 — 'Address of individual or company ... Street number and Street name'

fields.name: member1SharesTakenUp
fields.label: Member — number of shares taken up

fields.name: member1IsBeneficiallyHeld
fields.label: Member's shares — beneficially held?
fields.description: Beneficially held means the owner of the shares is entitled to the direct benefit from them (e.g. dividends); shares held as trustee/nominee/on account of another are non-beneficially held.

fields.name: applicantCapacity
fields.label: Capacity of applicant
fields.sourceRef: Declaration by applicant — 'Capacity of applicant: Individual / Corporation / Agent for individual or corporation'

fields.name: applicantName
fields.label: Name of applicant (or agent)
fields.sourceRef: Declaration by applicant — 'Provide family and given names OR corporation name (include ACN/ARBN if applicable)' / 'Name of agent'

documents.label: Signed declaration by applicant
documents.sourceRef: Declaration by applicant — 'I/we apply for registration of a company on the basis of the information in this form ... The information provided in this application and in any annexures is true and correct at the time of signing.' plus 'Signature of applicant'

documents.label: ASIC company registration fee
documents.sourceRef: business.gov.au live guidance: 'The fee is $636 from 1 July 2026 ($611 until 30 June 2026).' This document models the post-1-July-2026 amount.

steps.title: State of registration and company name
